frameworks.name: ingredients of intelligence
frameworks.one_line: Lake et al.'s list of humanlike-intelligence ingredients, augmented here with autonomy.

frameworks.name: model-based reasoning
frameworks.one_line: Endorsed as essential building block for humanlike agents.

frameworks.name: autonomous learning
frameworks.one_line: Agents that build and exploit their own internal models with minimal hand-engineering.

core_question: What additional ingredient beyond Lake et al.'s list is needed to scale toward humanlike intelligence in domains without ready-made formal models?
key_assumptions: Model-based reasoning is essential for humanlike intelligence
Real-world complexity often lacks ready-made formal models
Hand-engineering does not scale to open-ended domains
manipulations: Conceptual addition of autonomy to Lake et al.'s ingredient list
key_findings: Several existing agents already exhibit progress toward autonomous humanlike abilities
Outstanding challenges remain for fully autonomous model-building agents
interpretations: Autonomy — agents that build and exploit their own internal models — offers the best route to scaling toward real-world humanlike intelligence
limitations_noted: Outstanding challenges in autonomous agent construction remain unresolved
